The ELM specifies an inclusion age range of 60–75 years, whereas current USPSTF guidelines recommend colorectal cancer screening for average‑risk adults starting at age 45 (or 50 in some guidelines). Additionally, the “Has Recent Colorectal Screening” check limits recent screening to the past 2 years; however, screening modalities have different recommended intervals (e.g., colonoscopy every 10 years, FIT every 1 year). These discrepancies make the clinical parameters in the ELM not fully aligned with contemporary evidence‑based guidelines.

The BMI threshold for obesity is set at 35 kg/m², whereas most clinical guidelines recommend screening for prediabetes in patients with a BMI ≥30 kg/m² (obesity). This value is higher than the standard threshold and may exclude eligible patients.
